mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero and CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, the market reads policy as supportive and discount rates as suppressed, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if inflation re-accelerates enough to force a repricing of policy despite low current rates.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered periods with FEDFUNDS near zero and flat CPIAUCSL produce negative 20d SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the sign flips after inflation surprises.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening household income growth and rising recession risk, so equities should underperform over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when PAYEMS is also declining, confirming broad labor market deterioration.
failure_condition: Rejected if labor-deterioration triggers are followed by positive SPX 20d returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS is falling.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in RSAFS reflects stronger final demand and revenue expectations, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is falsified if sales growth rises while equity returns remain persistently weak.
failure_condition: Rejected if rising RSAFS is associated with negative or zero median 60d SPX returns over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and HOUST both rebound from recessionary lows, it signals cyclical re-acceleration in output and construction activity, which should lift equities over the next month. The mechanism fails if industrial recovery is not accompanied by improving market returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months and HOUST rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Rejected if combined rebounds in INDPRO and HOUST do not outperform baseline SPX 20d returns, or if either series rising alone explains the effect better than the joint condition.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in FEDFUNDS from emergency lows increases the equity discount rate and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is already firm, because inflation makes further tightening more likely.
failure_condition: Rejected if rate-hike triggers are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if high inflation does not strengthen the negative effect.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST signals improving housing demand, construction employment, and balance-sheet confidence, which should support equities over the next week. The effect fails if housing starts improve only because of rebound noise after an extreme collapse.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases for 2 straight monthly observations
failure_condition: Rejected if two-month increases in HOUST are not followed by above-baseline 5d SPX returns, or if the relationship reverses in recovery windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies a labor-market break that should trigger risk-off pricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is falsified if the labor deterioration does not translate into weaker equity performance.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint condition of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 20d SPX returns more often than chance.

mechanism_narrative: Weak or falling GDPC1 growth signals that aggregate output is slowing, which should hurt earnings expectations and lower S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is expected to be strongest when RSAFS is also soft, confirming broad demand deceleration.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 q/q annualized growth falls below 1%
failure_condition: Rejected if low-growth GDP triggers are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if RSAFS does not improve predictive power beyond GDPC1 alone.
